# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cicd security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Deploy on Pull Request

on:
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

env:
  GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

jobs:
  build-and-deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'
      
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      
      - name: Build and deploy
        env:
          PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
          PR_DESCRIPTION: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
        run: |
          echo "Deploying feature: ${PR_TITLE}"
          echo "Commit message: feat(${PR_TITLE}): ${PR_DESCRIPTION}"
          npm run build
          npm run deploy
      
      - name: Send notification
        uses: dawidd6/send-notification@v1
        env:
          MESSAGE: "Deployed feature: ${PR_TITLE}"
          DESCRIPTION: "${PR_DESCRIPTION}"
